sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with ankle pain and a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She reports that she slipped on ice two days ago and twisted her left ankle, which is more on the outside of her hip, causing pain, swelling, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, but it has provided only a little relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, and she denies pain in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She has not experienced any bleeding and has a swollen ankle but no redness.
The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure and is currently taking metformin, with stable blood sugars and regular self-monitoring.
She is due for an eye exam and has an appointment scheduled.
Her blood pressure is 120/70, and she is taking norvasc.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is taking metformin and norvasc.
Review of systems: The patient denies any pain or symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
Metformin, norvasc
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient has a normal neck exam, lungs, and heart exam, with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that is unchanged from before.
The belly exam is normal.
On the ankle exam, there is tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling, redness, and pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is likely suffering from an ankle sprain, and I will order an X-ray to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; Diabetes; High blood pressure
Plan:
I will order an X-ray of the ankle and put the patient in a splint.
I will prescribe naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control and instruct the patient to elevate the ankle, apply ice, and avoid weight-bearing until the X-ray results are available.
If the X-ray shows a fracture, I will refer the patient to orthopedics.
I will also refill the norvasc prescription and order baseline labs, including a hemoglobin A1c, to monitor the patient's diabetes.
The patient is to continue taking metformin and attend her scheduled eye exam.
Follow-up: The patient is to follow up with me after the X-ray results are available, and if there is no fracture, she will be instructed to remove the splint and start partial weight-bearing.
